reviews.review_title: Updated: tried it again after some time
reviews.review_description:
I got a response from developers saying they fixed all the issues. I went back to check it out, my progress had been wiped clean, which was fine. I'd probably start over anyway. But now, I can't do anything. The computer won't let me enter the amount of change a person gets so I can't check anyone out. Meaning I can't progress after the first customer! 

After trying again a month later, everything is working fine now. I'm obsessed with Supermarket simulation games. I own every single one. I've tried every one that's been put out on quest. This is okay. It has a decent amount of products. The checking out process could be better. All you do is type in the change. It starts at small until you're giving people a thousand dollars backin change. You dont actually touch money or debit cards like some of the other games. I do like the leaderboard where you race with other people for the highest sales. I won't play it much, but at least it is playable unlike several of the other supermarket games that are disasters.

The game is fun and many things could still be added, it has bugs that I hope can be fixed, a tip when you can't grab the products that the client leaves you on the table, change your height from settings, I still haven't discovered what to do when someone steals something from you, I just run them but it's fun, ultimately I give it 4 stars and I hope the developers improve some bugs and of course add more things.

Very bad game, there is no simple options as change the turn from skips to smooth and also the game itself got a lots of bugs and improvements. Definitely asking for refund after playing for like 20 mins. Didn’t enjoy at all.

Update: 
The money glitch is still there. It is simple calculations...

Just tried Minimarket Simulator and it is WAY WAY better. AND they know how money works.. I'm looking forward to see if this will ever get as good as that game. 


————————————

This looks really promising. BUT there’s a huge money glitch that makes the game unplayable by now.

You buy a product for $8 sell it for $10. Then the game for some reason just inserts the $2 profit into your account and not the $10. This makes no sense and means you are not able to restock after playing for 5 minutes.

developer_response.body:
-Sorry to hear that, that was a visual glitch. This issue has been resolved in Version 0.0.5.3
You should definitely try other supermarket simulators as well. Each game has its own personality and unique features.  I hope you will enjoy our game too. 😄

reviews.review_description: I received a key for free from a giveaway on Facebook. First off thank you all for the key I was super excited for this game. I think it’s great currently and I’m eagerly waiting for more features to come! The only thing I’d complain about is sometimes items during scanning items are too far away and it becomes challenging to grab them to scan. Besides that it’s a wonderful game and has such a beautiful city outside the store. Thank you devs again for the key and I can’t wait to hop back in the game!

I love a good simulator and I am loving this so far
The thought of running my own super store really excites me, I’m early doors into the game but I can see the potential already, I stocking my shelves and patiently waiting on my local customers, I hope there is good cctv to stop the shop lifters.

I want to make lots of money and open up a chain.
Having a lot of fun so far thank you to the devs.

This may be an absolute delight for fans of business management games as it blends the thrill of running your own store with some fun, The core loop of buying, stocking, and selling products seems to be quite engaging even though I have only dipped my toes in it. From selecting the perfect items to managing customer flow and expanding your shop, the game kept me hooked with trying to get upgrades and pay off my loans. 
The game strikes a perfect balance between challenge and relaxation as it’s easy to jump into and play at your own pace, with enough complexity to keep you thinking without being overwhelming. A must-try for anyone who loves simulation games!
A really fun little title with loads of room for the devs to build in new features - looking forward to seeing this evolve!

This is an interesting little title.
Start off with the basics, slowly building your shop into a bigger and better store.

Has a good grind and progression.

As you go on, buying more shelves, better stock, more stuff.. even improving the inside and outside of your store.
You can even hire shop staff and cashier staff to work for you.

You get targets to meet and levels to reach, to unlock these extra items.

Get stock delivered, place stock, move your shelves about.. then open the store to your wonderful customers. 
Make as much profit you can before the store close time. 

At first you have to staff the till yourself, running about stocking shelves, ordering in the most profitable items, and serving the customers at the till asap, to keep them happy. Make sure you give them the correct change! lol

I think there is also more to this game than what I have seen so far.

Maybe a bit more variety to the customers would help, as at one point I had a shop full of clones.. all looked the same… but it still served the purpose.

Maybe add in fridges and freezers to put drinks and ice cream into.
Maybe add a stock list, so can see what you have in stock at a glance.
Also a better way to scroll up and down through the inventory you can purchase. 

Very enjoyable and has that “just one more” feel to it… and at a great price too!

The game is about what I expected it to be. Just a very stress free casual job-simulating game. I've played it for a few short sessions (no music, no talking.. short sessions are all that's bearable), and though I I love that there are no time limits customer, I do hope the game opens up with a touch more to do and/ or get done. Though what is here is still good for a casual and relaxing session of play in short bursts.
I do wish the customers had voices and spoke what they say rather than quick text bubbles, but that's not a huge issue either I guess. It would add life to a very empty, if not mind numbing and lonely feeling game though.
One issue that is mbigger and REALLY hampers my enjoyment of the main and about only gameplay is grabbing the customers items to scan them being made WAY more difficult (if not straight up awkward) by the fact that your head CAN'T GO OVER THE COUNTER AT ALL for whatever reason. My arms aren't extremely long, so when I'm reaching for items and trying to reach over to the scanne which is too far away,, my entire body is getting pushed back because of the UNNECESSARY invisible wall that is over the entire counter you spend 99% of your game time behind that your headset can't get past!
So this means no looking down or reaching over the counter outside of where your arms can get to. And the item scanner is a bit too far up in an unnatural spot, so that should be moved in closer to you if anything...

Devs, fix this issue PLEASE! Grabbing items and reaching for the scanner is literally 99% of the ENTIRE GAME, it should not be THIS awkward... All that would be needed is removing whatever invisible wall you have on the counter that keeps the headset (your entire head) from going over it, which keeps you from even being able to look down unless you want to be pushed back in-game...
This ruins my enjoyment and will very likely lead to a refund (a warning to anyone wanting to buy this game if you've read my complaint)... 

I truly hope the developers keep patching & updating this game, though I've little faith in that. Still on the fence on if I'll keep this game as it is currently (leaning towards no..) but the premise and some of the execution shows promise. It just could be much better with some more work and basic smoothing out was put into it...
Definitely needs more time in the oven if it's not just the team not caring, not having the budget, or not having the skill...

I heard about this game on a facebook group I'm in and was intrigued straight away as I've played other shop simulators as a new game it's good with a few minor bugs removed this could be great I love it played about an hour had to force myself of love watching out for the thieves that's a great Addison thank you devs
